problem.title: Wired, tired, and running on cortisol
problem.sub: Most mornings start in fight-or-flight — screens, caffeine, and stress before your feet hit the floor. Your nervous system never gets a clean reset.

pains.title: Foggy focus
pains.text: You push through the morning half-awake, reaching for a third coffee just to think straight.

pains.title: Flat mood
pains.text: Motivation dips and the whole day feels heavy before it even begins.

pains.title: Stuck in stress
pains.text: A racing mind and shallow breathing keep your body locked in high alert.

pains.title: Slow recovery
pains.text: Training, travel, and late nights pile up with nothing to help you bounce back.

problem.bridge: Deliberate cold exposure paired with slow, controlled breathing is one of the simplest ways to flip that switch. RX SUBZERO puts both in your hands.

how.title: Three steps to your reset

steps.title: Fill & set up
steps.text: Add cold water and ice to the silicone basin. Unfold it in seconds on any counter — the drain rack keeps everything tidy.

steps.title: Breathe & submerge
steps.text: Take slow breaths through the built-in tube, lower your face, and let the cold do the work. Start with 20–30 seconds.

steps.title: Rise & reset
steps.text: Come up, breathe, and feel the wave of calm, alert energy. Repeat for 2–3 rounds, then drain and fold it flat.

how.tube_text: A medical-grade snorkel tube lets you keep your whole face submerged while you breathe slow and steady — the key to staying relaxed in the cold instead of gasping. Train your breath and your cold tolerance at the same time.
how.img_ph: Overhead: hands lowering a face into the RX SUBZERO basin

items.a: The first few seconds are intense, then it settles. Breathing slowly through the tube keeps you calm and in control — start with short 20–30 second dips and build from there.

items.q: How cold should the water be?
items.a: Most people aim for roughly 10–15°C (50–59°F) with a few handfuls of ice. Colder is not better — consistency matters far more than extremes.

items.q: How long should a session be?
items.a: Short and regular wins. Try 2–3 rounds of 20–60 seconds. Never push past comfort, and always finish by warming up naturally.

items.q: How does the breathing tube work?
items.a: The removable snorkel-style tube lets you keep your face submerged while breathing slow and steady above the water — the key to staying relaxed instead of gasping.

items.q: Is it easy to clean and store?
items.a: Yes. Rinse the silicone basin and tube, air-dry, and fold it flat to just a few centimeters. The lift-out rack makes draining simple.

Cold exposure is not for everyone. If you have a heart condition, high or low blood pressure, are pregnant, or have any medical concern, please consult your doctor first. Never practice cold immersion alone if you're new to it, and never in a way that risks your airway.
We ship fast with tracking, and every order is backed by our 60-night guarantee. If it's not for you, returns are simple and refunds are full.

finalcta.title: Meet the cold on your terms
finalcta.sub: Clearer mind, steadier mood, faster recovery — two minutes at a time. Your daily reset is waiting.
